title: Albizia isenbergiana
what_description: Albizia isenbergiana is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, native to tropical and subtropical regions of Africa and Asia. It is a deciduous tree or shrub, growing up to 15 meters tall, with a broad, flat crown and a straight, columnar trunk. The leaves are bipinnate, composed of 4-6 pairs of pinnae, each with 4-8 pairs of leaflets that are elliptical or ovate in shape. The inflorescences are axillary, consisting of 10-20 white or pale yellow flowers, each with 5 petals and 10 stamens. The fruit is a flat, papery pod, 10-20 cm long, containing 2-4 seeds.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Albizia isenbergiana are inflammation and hypertension, which it reduces through its flavonoid and alkaloid compounds. Albizia isenbergiana has also been found to lower blood sugar levels, making it beneficial for individuals with diabetes, and has been studied for its potential to inhibit the proliferation of cancer cells. Its anxiolytic properties help alleviate anxiety, and it has been shown to reduce rheumatism symptoms by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Albizia isenbergiana has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including gastritis and gastrointestinal issues, by reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of the gut lining, and it has also been found to be effective in reducing fever. Additionally, it has been used to treat skin conditions, including eczema, and skin infections, and has been shown to have a calming effect on the body to relieve stress.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Albizia isenbergiana are its antimicrobial activity, which prevents the growth of pathogenic microorganisms, its antioxidant properties, which protect cells from oxidative damage, and its anti-inflammatory effects, which reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. The analgesic properties of Albizia isenbergiana make it effective in relieving pain, while its antifungal activity prevents fungal infections. The bioactive compounds responsible for these properties include flavonoids, which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activity, and saponins, which have antimicrobial and antifungal effects. Additionally, volatile oils present in the plant contribute to its analgesic and antimicrobial properties. The combination of these bioactive compounds enables Albizia isenbergiana to exhibit its medicinal properties and provide therapeutic benefits.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Albizia isenbergiana are flavonoids, albizin, phenolic acids, saponins, and triterpenoids. These compounds exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties that contribute to the plant's medicinal value. Albizia isenbergiana contains flavonoids such as kaempferol and quercetin, which have been shown to possess antiviral and anti-cancer activities, as well as albizin, a flavonoid glycoside with hepatoprotective properties. The plant's phenolic acids, including protocatechuic acid and ferulic acid, have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, while its saponins have been reported to have immunomodulatory and anti-cancer effects. Triterpenoids, specifically oleanolic acid and ursolic acid, found in Albizia isenbergiana, have been demonstrated to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as hepatoprotective and anti-cancer activities.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Albizia isenbergiana are various and include Alibizia tea, made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water, infusion, a liquid extract of the leaves, flowers or bark in cold water, tincture, a concentrated liquid extract of the plant in a solvent such as ethanol, decoction, a liquid made by boiling the bark, leaves or roots in water, and salve, a topical application made from the plant's leaves, flowers or bark mixed with a carrier oil for skin conditions. Albizia isenbergiana is used in traditional medicine due to its antimicrobial properties and ability to treat various health conditions. The tincture is often used for its ability to reduce fever and relieve respiratory issues. The salve is used topically to treat skin irritations and wounds, while the decoction is used to treat gastrointestinal issues. These preparations have been traditionally used in various cultures for their medicinal properties.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Albizia isenbergiana improperly are experiencing nausea and vomiting due to its emetic properties, which can cause stomach irritation and lead to gastrointestinal issues such as diarrhea. This is because the plant's roots and leaves contain a range of toxic compounds, including glycosides and alkaloids, that can cause adverse reactions when ingested in excess or without proper preparation. Additionally, improper use of Albizia isenbergiana can trigger allergic reactions, ranging from mild skin irritation to anaphylaxis in severe cases, due to the plant's potential to cause histamine release. The severity of these side effects can be exacerbated by factors such as individual tolerance, dosage, and method of preparation. It is essential to consult with a qualified healthcare professional or experienced practitioner before using Albizia isenbergiana to minimize the risk of adverse reactions.

taxonomy: Albizia isenbergiana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes. It belongs to the subfamily Caesalpinioideae and the genus Albizia. The genus Albizia is a group of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Albizia isenbergiana is often found in the wild and is valued for its ornamental value. It is a deciduous tree with compound leaves and produces showy, fragrant flowers.
